text.title: Journey Mapping Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example: e-commerce purchase journey for a mid-market persona, demonstrating evidence citations, sharp emotional dips, and backlog-linked opportunities.

section.title: E-commerce purchase journey — Sarah, mid-market buyer

example.title: Stage × row matrix excerpt
example.p: Scope: persona Sarah; start "first ad impression"; end "first product use".
Stages: Discover → Research → Decide → Purchase → Receive → Use.
Emotional arc with sharp dip at Purchase: 3 → 4 → 2 → 2 → 2 → variable.

example.title: Purchase stage row
example.p: Actions: complete checkout form (evidence: ANL-checkout-2026-05). Touchpoints: web (evidence: ANL-funnel). Thoughts: "what is the final price?" (INT-P3). Emotions: anxious, score 2, evidence INT-P3. Pain: hidden fees at last step (INT-P3, INT-P7); long form (ANL-checkout). Opportunities: simplify to single page (BL-411); show total cost breakdown earlier (BL-412).

example.title: Where 'no data' is correct
example.p: If no analytics or interview evidence exists for "thoughts" at the Receive stage, the cell carries literal "no data" with evidence "no data". This becomes a research gap to fill, not an invention.

section.title: Output skeleton
section.p: The artefact follows the schema in content/02-output-contract.xml and the template at templates/journey-map.md. Reviewer signs off only after validate-journey-mapping.py exits 0.
